target.title: feat(memory): add RRF hybrid fusion mode for memory search
target.body:
Adds configurable fusion strategy (weighted | rrf) for memory search via agents.defaults.memorySearch.query.hybrid.fusion. Default: weighted (preserves existing behavior). RRF uses reciprocal rank fusion with configurable k parameter for combining semantic and keyword results.

## Summary

- **Problem:** Hybrid memory search always merges semantic + keyword results with a weighted score blend. This can be brittle when vector and BM25 scores live on different scales or distributions.
- **Why it matters:** Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) is a standard IR technique that merges rankings instead of raw scores, which handles score-scale mismatches better than weighted combination in some workloads.
- **What changed:** Adds an optional hybrid fusion mode (`weighted` | `rrf`) controlled by config, defaulting to `weighted` so existing deployments are unchanged. Implements RRF in the bundled `memory-core` extension and documents behavior in a contract doc.
- **What did NOT change:** Default fusion remains `weighted`. No change to embedding providers, memory storage format, or non-hybrid search paths.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes # N/A — happy to open an issue first if maintainers prefer design discussion
- Related # N/A
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A (feature addition)

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: RRF vs weighted ordering + tie-break/deterministic behavior
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Unit tests cover the fusion math directly without requiring a running gateway or real corpus
- Existing test that already covers this: Extended existing hybrid test suite with 7 RRF-specific cases

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config key: `agents.defaults.memorySearch.query.hybrid.fusion` with values `"weighted"` (default) or `"rrf"`.
- No behavior change unless users opt into `"rrf"`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[semantic results] + [keyword results] -> [weighted score blend] -> [merged list]

After (default, unchanged):
[semantic results] + [keyword results] -> [weighted score blend] -> [merged list]

After (fusion: "rrf"):
[semantic results] + [keyword results] -> [reciprocal rank fusion (k=60)] -> [merged list]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node 22
- Model/provider: N/A (memory subsystem, model-independent)
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: `agents.defaults.memorySearch.query.hybrid.fusion: "rrf"`

### Steps

1. `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts`

### Expected

- 7 tests pass (hybrid fusion suite)

### Actual

- 7 tests passed

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (N/A — new coverage, all 7 pass)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: local vitest for hybrid fusion (`weighted` + `rrf`) via `hybrid.test.ts`
- Edge cases checked: tie-breakers, deterministic ordering, k parameter behavior
- What I did **not** verify: full `pnpm build` / `pnpm check` (local build fails on unrelated upstream type errors in `ui/` and `provider-catalog-shared.ts` — not touched by this PR), gateway live run against a large corpus

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Yes (optional new key, default preserves existing behavior)
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** RRF weighting/tuning may not outperform `weighted` on all corpora.
  - **Mitigation:** Default stays `weighted`; `rrf` is opt-in; contract doc describes formula and constants (`k=60`).

## Files (11)

1. `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts` — `weighted` vs `rrf` fusion in `mergeHybridResults`
2. `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts` — RRF ordering regression tests
3. `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts` — pass `fusion` into hybrid merge
4. `src/agents/memory-search.ts` — resolve `hybrid.fusion` into runtime memory-search config
5. `src/config/schema.base.generated.ts` — generated schema includes `hybrid.fusion`
6. `src/config/schema.help.ts` — help text for the new key
7. `src/config/schema.labels.ts` — UI/docs label
8. `src/config/types.tools.ts` — TS types for tool/config surface
9. `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts` — zod validation for agent runtime config
10. `src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` — quality test bump for help coverage
11. `docs/reference/memory-rrf-contract.md` — contract: inputs, formula (`k=60`), ordering guarantees

## Contributor Notes

- This is my first PR to OpenClaw.
- [x] AI-assisted: Yes — Claude Code + cursor for architecture guidance, code review, and PR preparation.
